narrative.context: You receive a lower mark than expected. You feel disappointed and embarrassed, and you want to avoid thinking about it.
narrative.prompt: How can emotion support better thinking right now?

options.label: Use the feeling to identify what to change
options.description: Acknowledge disappointment, then review errors and plan 2 specific improvements for the next assessment.

ei.rationale: Uses emotion as a signal to evaluate gaps and adjust strategy—facilitates learning-focused thinking.

options.label: Take a short break then reflect
options.description: Step away briefly to calm down, then decide what to review.

ei.rationale: Supports thinking by reducing overwhelm; the emotion is partly used to guide reflection.

options.label: Ruminate on what it means about you
options.description: Replay the result and compare yourself with others.

ei.rationale: Emotion narrows thinking into self-judgment rather than problem-solving.

options.label: Avoid everything related to the subject
options.description: Decide it’s pointless and disengage completely.

ei.rationale: Avoidance blocks learning; emotion is not used to support constructive cognition.
